notes: Full precedence (first wins): ~/.config/agents/skills/, ~/.agents/skills/, ~/.config/amp/skills/, .agents/skills/, .claude/skills/, ~/.claude/skills/, then plugins, legacy toolbox directories, and built-in skills.
precedence_order: plugins, legacy toolbox directories, built-in skills
plugin_skill_loading: Amp's own plugins are TypeScript modules (PluginAPI exposes registerTool/registerCommand/on/experimental.createAgent) with NO skill-registration method — they cannot ship skills. The 'Plugins' entry in the skill precedence list refers to Amp reading skills bundled inside Claude Code plugin caches (~/.claude/plugins/cache/<owner>/<plugin>/<version>/skills/). Codex-native plugin caches (~/.codex/plugins/) are NOT read.

observed.method: Full resolved skill list dumped by the `amp` CLI (captured in docs/amp-skills.md), traced by file:// URI. Update 2026-07-05: `amp skill list --json` emits {skills: [{name, description, baseDir}], errors} — machine-readable; baseDir is a file:// URI identifying the source root. Returned 135 (53 ~/.agents + 79 Claude plugin-cache + 3 builtin) vs 132 on 2026-07-04.
observed.notes: Confirms plugins in path = Claude Code plugin caches only. No skills loaded from ~/.codex/plugins/. Loaded fewer vercel skills (27) than the cache holds (45), consistent with name-collision dedup against higher-precedence dirs. Update 2026-07-05 (walk-up probes): scans .agents/skills + .claude/skills in cwd and EVERY ancestor dir — probes 1-3 levels above the git root all loaded (git root is not a boundary; model as walk-to-filesystem-root). Does not scan below cwd; does not read .github/skills. Collision: dedup, nearest dir wins; same level .agents beats .claude.

notes: Also supports enterprise and plugin scopes. Same-name precedence is enterprise > personal > project. Plugin skills are namespaced (plugin-name:skill-name) so they cannot conflict. Project skills also load from .claude/skills/ in every parent directory up to the repository root.
plugin_skill_loading: Plugins bundle a skills/ directory (each skill a <name>/SKILL.md); skills are auto-discovered when the plugin is installed and namespaced plugin-name:skill-name so they never collide with the enterprise/personal/project chain. Verified loaded on this machine.

observed.method: Bogus-key debug harness: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=bogus claude -p hi --debug --debug-file <f> — auth fails after skill discovery (exit 1, no model call); debug log prints the scan set (Loading skills from: managed/user/project=[...]) + per-scope counts, and the failed run's transcript (~/.claude/projects/<munged-cwd>/*.jsonl) contains the full skills attachment as name: description lines. Isolation probes at 7 tree levels x 5 flavors + global roots + collisions (v2.1.201).
sources_loaded: 53 user from ~/.claude/skills/ (mostly symlinks into ~/.agents/skills/ plus real ui-* directories) + 10 legacy ~/.claude/commands = 63 skill-dir commands
80 plugin skills from ~/.claude/plugins/cache/ (compound-engineering 39, claude-plugins-official/vercel 28, claude-plugins-official/valtown 9, openai-codex 3, impeccable 1)
26 bundled skills in the binary ('Sending 144 skills via attachment' from a clean non-repo dir)
observed.notes: Isolation-probe confirmed (2026-07-06): does NOT read ~/.agents/skills/ or ~/.config/claude/skills/ — shared content arrives only via the ~/.claude/skills symlink farm (symlinks followed, project scope too). Project flavor is .claude/skills ONLY (no .agents/.agent/.github/.codex). Walk-up: cwd to git root inclusive; with no .git anywhere the walk continues to the filesystem root (undocumented). --add-dir <dir> loads <dir>/.claude/skills (exact dir, no walk) as a separate 'additional' scope. Collisions: no name dedup in the prompt (both copies listed, most-specific first); invocation winner is nearest project dir within scope, and USER beats PROJECT across scopes (matches documented personal > project). Directory name beats frontmatter name:. No trust gate. --bare skips skill-dir discovery; managed root /Library/Application Support/ClaudeCode/.claude/skills scanned but absent here (untested).

notes: Scopes are REPO, USER, ADMIN (/etc/codex/skills), and SYSTEM (bundled with Codex). For repositories, Codex scans .agents/skills in every directory from the current working directory up to the repository root. If two skills share the same name across scopes, Codex does not merge/override them - both remain available in skill selectors, so the order below reflects discovery order, not override precedence.
plugin_skill_loading: Codex plugins (managed via `codex plugin`, listed with `codex plugin list`) bundle a skills/ directory whose skills load when the plugin is enabled — a 5th skill source on top of the documented REPO/USER/ADMIN/SYSTEM scopes. There is NO `codex skills` list subcommand, BUT `codex debug prompt-input` (undocumented, found 2026-07-05) renders the model-visible prompt locally with the fully-resolved skills catalog — a '### Skill roots' alias table plus '### Available skills' with rN/<name>/SKILL.md paths. No model call, context-sensitive to cwd.

observed.method: `codex plugin list` (authoritative for enabled plugins) plus directory enumeration.
sources_loaded: 5 SYSTEM from ~/.codex/skills/.system/ (skill-creator, plugin-creator, skill-installer, openai-docs, imagegen)

enabled plugins: documents, pdf, spreadsheets, presentations, template-creator (openai-primary-runtime), browser (openai-bundled), vals (valtown, ~10 skills), github (openai-curated) — skills resolve under ~/.cache/codex-runtimes/ and ~/.codex/.tmp/, NOT ~/.codex/plugins/cache/
observed.notes: Codex reads ~/.agents/skills/ natively. Documented scope list omits the plugin source entirely. Update 2026-07-05 (isolation probes on v0.140.0 + openai/codex 0.142.5 source): walk-up scans cwd and every ancestor up to and INCLUDING the project root (nearest project_root_markers match, default .git; configurable; empty array = cwd only) — probes above the git root did not load. Reads BOTH .agents/skills and .codex/skills in-repo (latter under-documented); does NOT read in-repo .claude/skills. NO name-collision dedup: same-named skills at two levels or across flavors both appear (dedup is by physical path only; sorted by scope rank Repo > User > System > Admin). Deprecated ~/.codex/skills (user) still scanned. Roots scanned recursively to depth 6; directory symlinks followed.

notes: Also reads legacy compatibility directories: .claude/skills/ and .codex/skills/ (project), and ~/.claude/skills/ and ~/.codex/skills/ (global). Skills are not currently discovered outside the root .cursor/skills directory in monorepos. Precedence when a skill name collides across locations is not documented.
precedence_order: prompt sort tiers: builtin (~/.cursor/skills-cursor) -> user home -> workspace -> plugin
no name-keyed dedup found in source — colliding names likely coexist (unverified)
plugin_skill_loading: Cursor plugins (a directory with .cursor-plugin/plugin.json — distinct from VS Code editor extensions) bundle a skills/ directory whose skills appear in the 'Agent Decides' section and can be invoked with /skill-name. None installed on this machine.

observed.method: Directory enumeration (no non-interactive resolved-list command; the TUI requires workspace trust).
observed.notes: Reads ~/.agents/skills/ natively plus the legacy ~/.claude/skills/ and ~/.codex/skills/ globals. Update 2026-07-05 (cursor-agent 2026.07.01-41b2de7, bundle source + one headless --print --trust --mode ask probe call): anchor is the WORKSPACE DIR (= cwd or --workspace) with no walk in either direction — intermediate dir, git root, above-root, and below-cwd probes all failed to load; all four flavors (.cursor/.agents/.claude/.codex skills) loaded at cwd. The .claude/.codex legacy pair is gated by allow_third_party_plugin_imports (server-delivered team-policy field; enabled here). User scope mirrors: ~/.cursor/skills, ~/.agents/skills (+ gated ~/.claude, ~/.codex); builtins at ~/.cursor/skills-cursor. Trust-gated; --trust needed headless. Bundle contains a nestedExtensibilityService (downward ripwalk, globs */**/.cursor/**, */**/.agents/**) but it was inactive in the headless CLI run. No local query surface: 'cursor-agent skills' feeds the word to a live agent (paid trap). Precedence: sort-only tiers Builtin->UserHome->Workspace->Plugin, no name dedup in source; collision probe inconclusive.

notes: <repo>/.agent/skills/ is a compatibility location discovered for the .agent folder convention. Each skill lives in its own directory containing a SKILL.md. Precedence when a skill name collides across workspace/personal/compatibility locations is not documented. Only ~/.factory/skills/ (personal) and .agent/skills (compat) are documented; the `droid` binary ALSO reads ~/.agents/skills/, ~/.agent/skills/, and ~/.claude/skills/ globally (see observed) — all undocumented.
plugin_skill_loading: Factory plugins bundle a skills/ directory (skills are model-invoked automatically). None installed on this machine (0 Factory plugins).

observed.method: Three converging lines: (1) `droid` binary string analysis, (2) an isolation probe, and (3) droid's own self-investigation (docs/droid-skill-discovery-report.md).
sources_loaded: The droid binary constructs global skill roots via path.join(homedir(), X, 'skills') for X in {'.factory','.agents','.agent','.claude'} — verified in the decompiled fragment `join(H,".agents","skills")`.

~/.agents/skills/ is read DIRECTLY: an isolation probe (a uniquely-named skill created only in ~/.agents/skills/, with no symlink in ~/.claude or ~/.factory) moved the TUI count from Skills (53) to Skills (54).
observed.notes: CORRECTION of an earlier false negative: a prior pass grepped the binary for the slashed literal '.agents/skills' (0 hits) and wrongly concluded droid does not read ~/.agents/skills. The path is built by joining separate tokens ['.agents','skills'], so the slashed form never appears. droid DOES read ~/.agents/skills/ directly — confirmed behaviorally. All four global roots (.factory/.agents/.agent/.claude) are undocumented except ~/.factory/skills/. Update 2026-07-05 (v0.164.0, Skills (N) header via pty harness — 200x60 winsize, ANSI-stripped): project scope is a SINGLE ANCHOR, not an ancestor walk — inside a repo the anchor is the git root (cwd/intermediate/above-root probes did not load; git-root probes did), outside a repo the anchor is the exact cwd. Anchor flavors: .factory/skills, .agents/skills, .agent/skills (+3 confirmed); .claude/skills NOT read by the header scan set. Header count does not dedup names (collision counts as 2); runtime invocation winner unverified. Discrepancy flag: header home scan is .factory/.agent/.agents only (baseline exactly 53) — the earlier 4-global-roots-incl-~/.claude claim may reflect a separate legacy .claude importer code path (EM0, cwd->git-root walk) or be stale. Symlink caveat: header counter uses Dirent.isDirectory() (no symlink resolve).

notes: Full precedence (lowest to highest): built-in skills, extension skills, user skills, workspace skills. Within the same tier (user or workspace), the .agents/skills/ alias takes precedence over the .gemini/skills/ directory.
plugin_skill_loading: Gemini extensions (installed via `gemini extensions install`) bundle a top-level skills/ directory = the documented 'Extension Skills' discovery tier (above built-in, below user/workspace). No extension shipping skills is installed on this machine.

observed.method: Full resolved skill list from `gemini skills list --all`, traced by the reported Location: path.
sources_loaded: 2 built-in bundled in the @google/gemini-cli npm package (skill-creator, antigravity-support)
observed.notes: Does NOT read Claude/Codex plugin caches or ~/.claude/skills/ — only the .agents standard dir plus its own npm-bundled built-ins. Update 2026-07-05 (walk-up probes): project scope is cwd ONLY — cwd/.gemini/skills + cwd/.agents/skills, no walk-up (probes at git root and parent dirs did not load; bundle source uses path.join(targetDir,...) with no ancestor loop). Workspace skills load ONLY in trusted folders (~/.gemini/trustedFolders.json) — untrusted cwd makes `gemini skills list --all` silently omit all project skills. Collision: last-wins override with warning; load order builtin -> extensions -> ~/.gemini -> ~/.agents -> cwd/.gemini -> cwd/.agents, so project .agents/skills is highest.

notes: Project skills are stored in the repository; personal skills are stored in the home directory and shared across projects. Locations are presented as alternatives rather than a searched, prioritized list, and precedence on a name collision is not documented.
plugin_skill_loading: The cloud agent surface has NO plugin/extension skill contribution. (VS Code's chatSkills contribution point and the Copilot CLI's plugins do bundle skills, but those are different surfaces than the cloud agent.)

observed.method: Not locally introspectable — the cloud agent runs server-side against the repository plus its own cloud home, not this machine.
sources_loaded: none observable on this machine (~/.copilot/skills absent; repo .github/skills, .claude/skills, .agents/skills all absent)
observed.notes: The local ~/.agents/skills/ store is irrelevant to the cloud agent; only ~/.copilot/skills and in-repo dirs would matter, and none are populated here.

notes: .claude/skills/ and .agents/skills/ directories are supported for compatibility with Claude Code and the agent-standard convention. Project-local paths are walked upward from the cwd to the git worktree root and global paths are additionally loaded, but which location wins on a name collision is not documented.
precedence_order: nearest directory wins (project over global; deeper dir over shallower)
observed 2026-07-05 via collision probes — dedup to one entry per name
plugin_skill_loading: OpenCode plugins (local JS/TS in .opencode/plugins/ or npm packages) are for hooks, events, and custom tools ONLY — they do NOT contribute skills. Skills come solely from the six documented directories. (Third-party plugins exist that reimplement skill-loading as a tool, but that is the inverse — the native path does not ingest plugin skills.)

observed.method: Directory enumeration (no skills subcommand in the CLI).
observed.notes: Reads ~/.agents/skills/ natively plus ~/.claude/skills/ for Claude compatibility. Update 2026-07-05 (v1.17.9, isolation probes): OpenCode HAS a query surface — undocumented `opencode debug skill` emits JSON [{name, description, location, content}] with absolute SKILL.md paths ('<built-in>' sentinel for bundled), no model call, context-sensitive to cwd (pipe was flaky; redirect to file then parse). Global roots probe-confirmed: ~/.config/opencode/skills, ~/.opencode/skills (undocumented), ~/.agents/skills, ~/.claude/skills, +1 built-in (customize-opencode). Project flavors read: .opencode/skills, .agents/skills, .claude/skills; NOT read: .github/skills, .agent/skills. Walk-up: git-root-bounded inclusive (above git root not loaded); opencode.json-only boundary untested. Precedence: dedup to one — nearest dir wins; same level .agents > .opencode > .claude; project overrides global. No config gating — skills load with no opencode.json.

notes: Project skills are resolved from .pi/skills/ and .agents/skills/ in the cwd and ancestor directories, up to the git repository root (or filesystem root when not in a repo). ~/.agents/skills/ is shared with Claude Code / OpenAI Codex. On a name collision, Pi warns and keeps the first skill found, but the order in which locations are checked is not documented.
precedence_order: .agents/skills walk-up (project, nearest ancestor first, to git root)
first-wins name dedup over this order (observed 2026-07-05)
plugin_skill_loading: Pi packages (npm/git, installed via `pi install`) are a first-class skill source — via skills/ directories or a pi.skills entry in package.json — alongside the project/global dirs. The one installed package here (pi-cursor-sdk) ships no skills.

sources_loaded: 1 installed package (npm:pi-cursor-sdk) with no bundled skills
observed.notes: Reads ~/.agents/skills/ natively; no package- or ~/.pi-sourced skills currently loaded. Update 2026-07-05 (v0.80.3, isolation probes + source at ~/.opensrc): Pi HAS a query surface — RPC mode: printf '{"type":"get_commands","id":"1"}\n' | pi --mode rpc --offline --no-session returns JSONL whose data.commands includes each resolved skill as {name: 'skill:<name>', description, sourceInfo:{path, baseDir, scope, origin}}. No model call/key/network; cwd- and trust-sensitive. Global roots (trust-independent): ~/.pi/agent/skills ($PI_CODING_AGENT_DIR overridable) + ~/.agents/skills. Project roots (TRUST-GATED via ~/.pi/agent/trust.json; --approve trusts for the run): <cwd>/.pi/skills (cwd only, NO walk-up) and .agents/skills (walk-up cwd->git root inclusive; filesystem root if no .git). Package skills: npm/git/local packages from settings.json, via pi.skills manifest or conventional skills/ dir. Precedence high->low: project .pi > project .agents (nearest first) > ~/.pi/agent > ~/.agents > package; first-wins name dedup + physical-path dedup. Gotcha: the trust-need check scans .agents/skills ancestors UNBOUNDED while the loader is git-root-bounded, so a repo can demand trust for dirs the loader never reads.
